seoTitle: How to Record Merchant Fees in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Find out how to record merchant fees in QuickBooks Online accurately, so your payment processing costs are tracked separately from your actual sales income.
intro: Every time a customer pays by card, your processor takes a cut, and that fee needs its own line in your books. This guide explains how to record merchant fees in QuickBooks Online so your revenue and expenses stay clearly separated.

steps.heading: Set up a merchant fees expense category
steps.body: Create or confirm you have an expense account dedicated to merchant or processing fees.

steps.heading: Locate the transaction with the fee
steps.body: Find the deposit or payment entry where the merchant fee was deducted from the gross amount.

steps.heading: Split the transaction
steps.body: Separate the gross payment into the actual sales amount and the merchant fee, assigning the fee to its own category.

steps.heading: Save and review the entry
steps.body: Confirm the split is correct and save it so the fee appears properly in your expense reports.

faq.question: Why shouldn't I just record the net deposit amount?
faq.answer: Recording only the net amount hides how much you're actually paying in fees, which makes it harder to track true income and expenses.

faq.question: Are merchant fees tax deductible?
faq.answer: Merchant fees are typically a deductible business expense, but confirm with an accountant for your specific situation.

faq.question: Can I automate merchant fee recording?
faq.answer: Depending on your payment processor's connection to QuickBooks, some fees can be recorded automatically through bank feed rules.

faq.question: What account type should merchant fees use?
faq.answer: Merchant fees are usually categorized under a bank charges or merchant fees expense account.
